title: Influence Is Your Superpower
subtitle: The Science of Winning Hearts, Earning Trust, and Building Lasting Relationships
author: Michelle Tillis Lederman
themes: Influence is not manipulation — authentic influence produces better, lasting outcomes
Understanding the 7 universal principles of persuasion: reciprocity, consistency, social proof, liking, authority, scarcity, unity
Emotional intelligence as the core capability of influential communicators
Communication that builds trust vs. communication that destroys it
Building authentic relationships as the foundation of all long-term influence
When and how to apply each influence principle ethically
Influence in organizations — culture, leadership, and team dynamics
The science of persuasion backed by behavioral economics and neuroscience
Resistance triggers — what causes people to close off and how to work with them
First impressions matter: warm, genuine connections open doors that technique alone cannot
awards: 4 published books on communication and leadership
Clients include Fortune 500, nonprofits, universities, government agencies
Featured on major media: CBS, NBC, Fox, NPR, CNBC, New York Times, Forbes
excerpt: Influence Is Your Superpower is Michelle Tillis Lederman's science-backed guide to ethical persuasion in the workplace and beyond. Drawing on Cialdini's seven principles of influence and integrating emotional intelligence with decades of corporate consulting experience, Lederman shows that authentic influence — rooted in trust and genuine connection — consistently outperforms manipulation, both in outcomes and in durability. There are no shortcuts: the book offers a framework for developing influential communication as a durable, learnable skill built on understanding human psychology, managing resistance, and building relationships that last.
